title: Fix missing scrollbar on the press kit page

requirements.summary: Fix the PressKitPage so that the page is fully scrollable with a visible scrollbar, ensuring the root container and parent elements do not suppress vertical overflow
acceptanceCriteria: The /press-kit page is vertically scrollable and all sections are reachable by scrolling.
A scrollbar appears on the press kit page when content overflows the viewport height.
No CSS rule on the page root, body, or any wrapping container suppresses vertical overflow on the press kit route.
Scrolling behavior is unaffected on the landing page (/) and admin (/admin) routes.

file_operations.description: Review and fix the root container and wrapper elements to ensure they do not have overflow:hidden, overflow:clip, or h-screen/h-full constraints that would prevent scrolling. Ensure the page content is wrapped in a scrollable container that allows vertical overflow and displays a scrollbar when content exceeds viewport height.

Review the router outlet wrapper to ensure no overflow or height constraints are applied that would suppress scrolling on child routes. Verify that the root div does not have h-screen, overflow-hidden, or similar CSS that would prevent the press kit page from scrolling naturally.

Review global CSS rules for html, body, and #root to ensure no overflow:hidden, overflow:clip, or height:100vh/100% constraints are preventing vertical scrolling. Add or adjust CSS to explicitly allow vertical overflow and ensure the scrollbar is visible when content overflows.
